blocks.text: Explain the current file structure of this project

This is a VS Code extension that integrates the OpenCode AI agent into VS Code. Here's the high-level architecture:

## Core Structure

- **`src/extension.ts`** — Extension entry point; registers commands and the webview provider
- **`src/chat/ChatProvider.ts`** — Main orchestrator for the chat panel; delegates to services
- **`src/session/SessionManager.ts`** — Manages the opencode HTTP server lifecycle

## Chat Services

The chat panel is decomposed into focused services:

| Service | Responsibility |
|---------|---------------|
| `StreamCoordinator` | Manages concurrent AI streams |
| `MessageRouter` | Routes webview ↔ host messages |
| `TabManager` | Per-tab state and persistence |
| `RetryQueueService` | Message retry with backoff |

## Webview Layer

The webview (`src/chat/webview/`) is a standalone browser bundle:

- **`main.ts`** — Entry point; message dispatch and state management
- **`renderer.ts`** — Block-level rendering (text, tools, diffs, errors)
- **`composer.ts`** — Input area, slash commands, queue UI

The architecture follows a **thin-orchestrator** pattern — `ChatProvider` delegates to services rather than accumulating logic.
